---
description: "Language policy for this project: always respond in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) and localize end-user text to Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR). English in code is allowed, but not required."
applyTo: "**"
---
# Language And Localization Rules

## Assistant Response Language
- Write all assistant explanations and guidance in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR).

## Code Language
- English in source code is allowed, but not required.
- Follow the existing language conventions of each module/file when possible.
- Use English or Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) for identifiers (variables, functions, classes, types, constants, file names).
- Use Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) for code comments and documentation written for developers.

## Documentation Language
- Write project documentation in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) by default (README, requirements, technical docs).

## User-Facing Language
- Write all user-facing text in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR) with proper accentuation.
- This includes UI labels, placeholders, validation messages, alerts, notifications, and frontend fallback API error messages.

## Portuguese Text Quality
- Any text written in Portuguese (pt-BR) must use proper accentuation and orthography.

## Precedence
- If text is shown to end users, use pt-BR.
- If text is for developers or implementation details, use English.
